verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass transmembrane protein expressed on the surface of sperm. Its ectodomain is essential for sperm-egg fusion during fertilization. However, surface display is restricted to the male germline (spermatogenesis and mature sperm). The protein has an explicit TM domain and extracellular loops involved in gamete recognition, but its surface accessibility is confined to a narrow developmental / tissue lineage (germline). This meets the criterion for `tissue_restricted_surface`: a protein with its own membrane anchor (TM) whose surface display is restricted to a specialized cell-type lineage (male germline / reproductive tract).
